Это безумно странные ощущения, но однозначно с положительной коннотацией. 
Я никогда не увлекалась футболом, и он мне не был интересен как вообще спорт жанр игр. Скорее я фан шутеров, моб, и мморпг, где так или иначе происходит взаимодействие с командой и другими реальными ГЭЙмерами, а не болванчиками. И вот именно этот аспект, прямой конкуренции, постоянного контакта с энеми и тиммейтами, для меня делает эту игру прям привлекательной, что позалипать, и местами попотеть это милое дело. 

Так что, я однозначно рекомендую попробовать игру чувакам, с пулом интересов как у меня, и не фанатов футбольчика. 
Есть нихуевая вероятность что эта дура вам понравится, и вы втянетесь. 
Отдельный интерес вызывает то, что здесь реально, мать его, есть различные механики, и способы взаимодействия с мячом, трюками и т.д, кароче простор для скилла. Тут надо и попадать, и учитывать траекторию, знать как ведет себя игра, предиктить соперника и много других нюансов, которые вы прочувствуете уже после того как поймете, что хотите играть лучше. Кароче играть однозначно интересно, и местами это напоминает мобу, только без механики закупа :D

Будем честны REMATCH - это глоток свежего воздуха в мир так называемых спортивных симуляторов и надеюсь в будущем киберспорта. Я как великий ненавистник Фифы и тому подобных игр, был приятно удивлен и затянут в этот мир где все сводится к тому что надо просто "пинать" мяч.
Посмотрим на дальнейшие обновления и поддержку игры, но то что имеется на данном этапе очень и очень впечатляет.

Let's be honest, REMATCH is a breath of fresh air in the world of so-called sports simulators and, hopefully, in the future of esports. As a great hater of FIFA and similar games, I was pleasantly surprised and drawn into this world where everything boils down to simply "kicking" the ball.
We'll have to wait and see about future updates and game support, but what we have at this stage is very, very impressive.

The server issues and netcode problems are a pain in the ass and mess up at least every thrid game, while you either play with a bunch of apes that are too confused to pass the ball or you play against jesus christ and friends lol. Also having NO CROSSPLAY at launch in 2025 and not even having crossplay between Steam and GamePass is almost criminal. Because of that I had to buy the game to play with friends even though I already pay Microsoft a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of money. So... my frustration aside, I think this game has great potential and if the devs focus on the current issues and also add things like ranked for 3vs3 and 4vs4, a training mode maybe similar to Rocket League, clubs/clans or whatever you wanna call it and last but not least LET ME CHANGE THE CONTROLS ON MY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ PS5 CONTROLLER, I think it has the potential to be a great and fun game.

However the servers seem to get more unstable everyday, we've had countless d-syncs and crashes, the amount of times we've lost games to glitches, lag and the ball just phasing through the goal keeper is insane. 
In my opinion the fact they've also added micro transactions before the game properly launched and before fixing issues which should have been more or less sorted on day one is very big red flag. 

As the game stands right now I personally cannot recommend buying it at full price until they have fixed the fore mentioned issues with the game.

Update: The developers have no introduced FOMO mechanics in the season passes. This is rather sad to see as I genuinely hoped they would not stoop to those levels. 
Even though it seems the gameplay is a little better (smoothness, volleys, etc) the player base has become alot more toxic than when I left, it seems more players are leaving games early or trolling. A friend of mine joined a game in proccess and realised two other players left before him, even in the most sweaty competitive games I have never seen that and from what I've seen a punishment system still ins't inplemented. 

This game is pretty much dead to me at this point. Don't buy this game it's just a waste of time.

Игрушка классная! В нее прикольно играть, когда ничего не лагает и когда получается сыграться с командой. Красивые анимации, приятное управление на геймпаде (на клавомыше - полный кал). Но у нее есть куча недостатков:

- лаги/статтеры/коннект: в каждой 2-3 катке обязательно что-то пролагает, обязательно мяч из рук вратаря телепортируется в ворота, обязательно сломается лобби или все игроки станут невидимыми
- невозможность репортить/лайкать/мьютить игроков: периодически попадаются обиженки, которые нон стопом будут спамить good job, или кнопку пасса и руинить, и с этим ничего не сделать
- половина трофеев забагана: некритично конечно, но неприятно
- мало контента: весь сезонный пропуск закрывается за часов 15, после этого кроме гринда рейтинга в игре особо нет какой-то цели. А учитывая выше перечисленные проблемы, гринд ммров - глубоко бесполезное занятие, даже скорее вредное для нервов
- нет ботов или адеватной тренировки: первые часов 20 ужасно больно играть с теми, кто скипнул пролог (или его забыл) и пытается втянуться по ходу. Хочется адекватных тренировок для нубиков или матчей с ИИ
- ДОРОГХО

Rematch is a complete mess of a game and unless the devs make serious changes, it will never be successful. The biggest issue? They lump every single EU player into one giant server, and the result is absolutely disastrous. What I see on my screen is completely different from what other players are seeing — the desync is beyond insane.

You save an attack, but it still goes through. It’s like every player is living in a different version of the match. Competitive gameplay is impossible when the core mechanics are broken at this level. And don't expect it to get better — this isn't just a minor lag issue, it's a fundamental flaw in how the game is built.

Until they fix the way servers and matchmaking are handled, Rematch will stay in the gutter. It's frustrating, unfair, and completely kills any fun the game might have had. Avoid unless you enjoy losing to broken hit registration.

The current state of the game is how it should have launched 6 months ago when everyone felt the potential and the hype was real (around 90k players). [b]What went wrong?[/b] In my opinion it was [u]time.[/u]

Unfortunately, it had a very rough start with tons of game breaking bugs and unpolished mechanics that started to build up a lot of frustration after the honey moon phase faded. Those of us who stayed loyal to the game and believed in the future patches / roadmap witnessed how the player base shrunk to its current 1k to max. 5k concurrent players. I'm not sure what these numbers mean in other world regions, but in Europe I would be able to get 5v5 competitive matches fast (max. 1-2 mins of waiting) but it would be very often against and/or with the same players. Also all skills & ranks would be mixed to shorten the queue times, yet making the matches unbalanced and chaotic. I've had griefers on my team or full stack clans on the enemy team that in order to avoid and create a more enjoyable experience for myself, I would have to play at some another time. 

So in conclusion, I think this game suffered from either being released [u]too early[/u] and/or releasing patches & fixes [u]too slowly[/u] (e.g. basic things like report systems, punishing leavers, etc.).

However, I see how much the game has changed in a positive direction, so credit to the developers for not giving up and hopefully this game becomes more successful in the future. Also I do have to admit that while the game was at its prime I met a lot of new people and had lots of fun. If I could I would give this a neutral review, but I feel like the game is not ready to be recommended yet.

Long-time FIFA/FC/PES players will need time and patience to get used to the controls.

As with Pro Club or other team-based games, there will be frustrating moments with random players (and maybe even with yourself). Occasionally, the controls feel a bit clunky, and the hitboxes can seem awkward (skill issue, maybe? git gud).

Once you've learned and familiarized yourself with the controls, the gameplay is extremely fun and rewarding despite these issues. It's especially enjoyable with friends (I would think, I currently don't have friends to play with). The feeling of your skills improving significantly game by game is just *chef's kiss*.

Pre-level 5 queue times are quite atrocious, except for 3v3. Ranked queue times are a non-issue. Still, I think the game desperately needs cross-platform support so I can play with my console peasant friends. (The devs have said a cross-platform update is coming soon.)

The ranked/elo points calculation could better reflect your contributions to the game. Despite these minor issues, the game is a must-buy/must-play for football fans worldwide.

the concept is good, but the execution isn't.  i don't hate the game, but i think we all expected much more from the devs of sifu.  the multiplayer is the worst i've played in years, the playerbase is full of toxicity and rematch is probably one of the buggiest games i've seen in a long time.  

i play goalkeeper exclusively and i have to say the goalkeeper mechanics are also completely garbage.  you can even dive into your own net if your joystick is a tad bit to the back.  speaking of joysticks, there's a tiny - but very annoying - input delay on joysticks, which makes you jump up instead of to the side if you're too quick.  plus, the amount of unfixed overpowered techniques completely overwhelms a keeper.  

was excited to play a cool new football game, but after a few months, the player count dwindled to nearly a 50th of what it used to be, making it hard to get new players every match.  the moment you spend more than a few dozen hours actually playing the game, you start to understand how horrendous the state of the game is.  i'm surprised they didn't pull a splitgate 2 and went back into beta after all the bugs surfaced immediately. 

 imho, the devs released the game too early and then began to focus on the wrong problems.  the devlogs never mention anything relevant to the state of the game, instead trying to fix minor freeplay bugs, or tiny delays in desync.  

all in all, i don't recommend this to anybody who's never participated in an og cod lobby, 'cause from my experience, you get told to kill yourself more than anything else.  no "good jobs" or "tekkers" or whatever, just ♥♥♥♥-flinging insults at you all game long while playing like a newborn cockatiel.

As an aficionado of fast-paced, skill‑intensive sports simulations, I was drawn to Rematch’s elegant hybridization of arcade flair and tactical football. The core gameplay—one‑player control in 5v5 matches—is indeed a cerebral and kinetic pleasure.

Yet the frigid reality of its online experience threatens to render the entire endeavor impotent.

The chief ailment: inconsistent server stability and network synchronization. Despite post-launch patches intended to ameliorate desyncs and crashes , the phenomenon remains pervasive. My sessions are marred by jerked ball physics, phantom goalkeepers, and momentary disconnects—not as occasional lapses but as recurring impediments eroding the competitive integrity .

These grievances are not marginal—they narrate a systemic architecture that fails to provide reliable matchmaking or consistent host locality, even upon rematch instantiation 

When the game unpredictably transposes you to a distant server mid‑session, the ostensible UX genius underpinning Rematch collapses into dissonance. Real-time strategy and reflex-based coordination become moot when the simulation’s spatio‑temporal fidelity fluctuates so widely. Every stumble, every phantom blow, every mercurial glitch shatters the suspension of immersive gameplay.

It is evident that until Rematch receives not just cosmetic hot‑fixes but a rigorous overhaul of its netcode and server infrastructure—complete with regional servers, rollback/client‑prediction layers, and stability‑first architecture—its brilliance remains encased in brittle glass.

Conclusion:
In its current incarnation, Rematch is an exemplar of high‑concept squandered by infrastructural negligence. Its aesthetic and mechanical foundations may gleam, but the connective tissue—its servers and network engineering—are unreliable to the point of dysfunction. Beautiful in aspiration, ruinous in execution.

I want to really give this game a thumbs up but right now its in a rough state. I try to take the stance of its only season 0 (just like RL had its issues) so I will update my review once season 1 drops. 

-I think the priority system could use a little work as one person will have the ball and completely lose it just because someone sneezes on you. 
-There is a chance that you will randomly not be able to touch the ball and everything will pass through you, the only way to counter this is passing or shooting immediately no matter where you are on the field. (Heaven forbid your goalie when this happens and your whole team yells at you lol)
-I think goalie feels a little better than in the beta, but I think that you should only be able to dive or slide. I think the "catch boxes" for both are a little off. You could slide for the ball and its just a little to high or you could dive for the ball and its a little to low. tweaking this I think would make it feel a little better.
-The servers are hit or miss, sometimes they are great and sometimes it makes you want to uninstall on the spot. I know RL went through this too so i do believe they will get better. 

Overall I think this game is a strong 7/10, if they keep tweaking and working towards updates I do believe this game will continue to succeed. In this day and age though season 1 will be a big tell and I hope they are putting alot of focus in fixing the little things.

REMATCH is the perfect game if you love short, intense matches and emotional whiplash. You log in for 6 minutes of pure adrenaline, then log out to cry in peace. It’s like speed dating with violence.
Fast-paced, super enjoyable, and perfectly bite-sized. I love how I can hop in, get destroyed, and question my self-worth all in under 10 minutes.
BUT... playing on mouse and keyboard is like showing up to a sword fight with a pool noodle. Controller players move like ballet assassins while I'm here panic-tapping every key like I’m trying to launch nukes.

Still, 10/10 would queue up again and scream internally.

UPDATE 250905
A reporting system is now in place. Cross platform is live. And improvements have been made to netcode and gameplay. I now recommend the game because the biggest issues have been fixed.

---

This game has huge potential, but there are some things that needs to be addressed before I can give this a recommendation.

[b]Missing reporting system[/b]
A competitive 5v5 game needs a solid reporting system for teammates who are griefing or going AFK.

[b]Hit detection needs improvement[/b]
Sometimes the ball just goes through your player, and it can last an entire game if you are unlucky.

[b]Improve the netcode[/b]
This is very noticable when the replay doesn't match what you saw on your own screen. To my knowledge, Rocket League never had these issues despite it being way more fastpaced.

Игра в целом огонь! 🔥 Если, конечно, не считать одного "маааленького" нюанса — отсутствия скинов из Blue Lock. Но это же мелочи, правда? Зато у нас есть диванные "Неймаровые Месси", которые:

Наполнены эгоизмом до краёв,

Имеют в кармане сотню радуг (видимо, на удачу),

И свято верят, что они — второе пришествие Роналдиньо.

Типичная ситуация:
Счёт 0:4 (естественно, не в твою пользу). Ты на вратаре, отдаёшь пас такому "гению" в надежде, что он:
✅ За 10 секунд провернет свою "фирменную" комбинацию,
✅ Со словами "В этот раз точно получится!" обведёт всю команду,
✅ И забьёт тот самый "мегакрутой" гол...

Но по факту:
❌ Он просрёт мяч на первом же дриблинге,
❌ Ты вылетишь в лобби с криком "Курва ебане!!!",
❌ А он, скорее всего, уже пишет в чат: "Ну ты и лох, вратарь".

Вывод:
Если играть с адекватным тиммейтом — то всё шикарно. Но таких, увы, нужно ещё поискать.

Как можно улучшить игру? (список требований простого смертного)
Кнопка "Начать заново" в тренировке
А то приходится выходить-заходить, как будто это не футбол, а квест на выживание.

Карты из мастерской для тренировок
Хочется разнообразия, а не бесконечных ударов по воротам в одной и той же локации.

Коллаб с Blue Lock (или хотя бы скины)
Чтобы сразу было понятно, кто есть кто. Посмотрел на игрока — и сразу ясно: "Окей, этот чувак точно не профит".

Простые матчи с ботами
Иногда хочется потренироваться, а не нырять сразу в котел с кричащими тиммейтами.

Итог:
Если добавить этот базовый минимум, игра станет ещё лучше. А пока... держим кулаки за свою психику и надеемся на адекватных союзников. 🤞
EDIT: Hanno aggiunto le missioni e il gioco ha avuto dei cambiamenti sostanziali per darti un motivo per giocarci.
My therapist told me I needed to 'face my demons'. So I bought REMATCH. Now I'm just getting styled on by pixels and my ego is in the dirt. Thanks, I hate it, but also... one more round.

The core gameplay is genuinely fun and has a lot of potential, but the overall experience is dragged down by a wide range of issues that make it feel like an unfinished product.

First of it its recommended to play with a controller but the game would just not recognize my controller.
And the mouse and keyboard controls are terrible and clunky. Intercepting a ball feels like you're doing finger gymnastics.
Even the menu controls are bizarre. You use Q and D to scroll through main sections, but suddenly switch to A and E for subcategories. Why the heck is the layout diagonal? It makes no sense.

The UI is a mess. Some menus you navigate through Q and D or A and E, others require clicking through panels, it's a random mix and doesn't feel like a consistent logic. Which makes it disorganized and frustrating to navigate. They really need to pick one design or at least clearly seperate the different styles.

Queueing is broken. There's a button to cancel matchmaking, but pressing it just removes the button form the screen without actually taking you out of the queue. (This makes you really questiong if they even play tested the game)

And then there's the desync. Players and the ball frequently teleport, making some matches unplayable.

One of the most frustrating parts is how unreliable ball possesion is. The ball is supposed to stick to you when you run over it (at least thats what they teach you in the prolog), but often it just doesn't. Even if no one is near you, it might stay in place or randomly fall of while you're running, forcing you to turn around and pick it up again.

Sometimes, after scoring, there's a chance of triggering game-breaking bugs. 
For example:
- All players become invisible. You can just see the ball and yourself.
- You can no longer interact with the ball, except for shooting, completely ruining goalkeeping, since the ball passes right through you and you can't grab it.
- Everyone gets stuck in their starting animation, meaning you can't ready players actions or react properly, beacuse their movements aren't visible.

Worst of all, these bugs don't resolve after the next goal or round reset, they persist until the match ends.

A 3v3 - 5v5 online competitive, futuristic setting, no-rules arcade soccer game. Reminiscent of the days of NBA Jam and NFL Blitz.

Highly addictive game play that makes you want to come back for more. Take control of your own individual player and fully customize him/her. Down to victory animations and Home/Away stadiums.

As per feedback. Please improve net coding. Janky matches hurt the experience and can cause unnecessary match upsets. Also, please work on hit detection between player models fighting for ball or defense. Players in offensive animations clip through the ball in possession of the other team without reaction.

The game has room to improve and grow but is nevertheless a highly competitive and addictive game.

Conceptually, it is fire. The only problem this game has is that its developers are greedy clowns who rushed everything to monetize the game in every way they could, but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ forgot to at least try to do something for the game so it wouldn’t ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ look, play, and feel like a pre-alpha.
The game feels like it was stitched together by a few developers fresh out of online COVID-era courses, plus a sprinkle of AI assistance and some monetization expertise :). It runs like a beta at best, though honestly, it's missing features you'd expect even in early test builds.
Music? Basic, AI-generated lo-fi slop on repeat with about 4 tracks total lol. Prepare to hear the same thing on repeat forever.
UI? Colored boxes that look ripped from Windows 8. No chat, no forfeit option, can’t mute mic without diving into a clunky menu mid-match.
Netcode? Laggy, unsynchronized, frequent crashes. Scripted actions (which is like 97% of gameplay) half the time don’t even trigger properly.
Performance? Graphics like Minecraft but requires a PC built for GTA VII to play with decent FPS.
For now, I’m giving this wreck a 3/10 solely for its great concept. I want this game to succeed. But these greedy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ seriously need to get their act together and build something worthy of that potential. Currently, this game is actually in beta max (not pricewise or monetization options tho lol, of course).

Back in the beta the game crashed constantly for me, and the de-sync was a problem. But I bought the game a few weeks after release and all my issues I experienced have been fixed.
After a few dozen hours I have only encountered a very small amount of technical problems.
The game-play is great, one problem is how low the skill ceiling is for goalkeeper since there is too little tech at your disposal.

Even with this said I would recommend waiting for more updates since right now it is pretty content-dry.
One game-mode, you can choose between 3v3, 4v4, 5v5 and ranked 5v5 but that's it.
The battle-pass is alr, however progressing it is a bore since the only "challenge" to complete is a daily win.
The shop is why the game isn't ftp since the only products that require real money are the "bundles", and you gain your money back from the battle-pass.

I can however recommend getting the game and playing under 2 hours so you can decide if you want to refund or not. Especially if you're worrying about the crashes and de-syncs some people are experiencing

I would love to give a positive review of this game but there are some things that I think make this game bad in my personal opinion.
I will talk about the positives of the game it is indeed a very nice game with very enjoyable and fun gameplay the fact that there are no stats for the player I think it is a very good choice because it only has to do with the skills of each player and you get better because you learn the game and not because you uploaded some stats to your player.
I could play for hours because it is indeed an enjoyable game that is hard to find nowadays but there are some things that seem like red flags from the developers to me.
First of all my biggest complaint for me is that the game seems like it's not ready yet where you can play your game calmly you start having problems with the servers you see the ball teleport from one point to another the same with the players especially for a game that is based on personal skills and you need to be focused to be able to either attack or defend, seeing you score a goal unfairly because the ball teleported with the opponent especially in a ranked match I don't think it's very nice.
Another thing I wanted to comment on is that you are asking people 25€ for the game and it hasn't been well over a month since you released the game and it shows how much money rather than one or two stupid things to say you gave something away for free.
The fact that you ask someone for money to play your game and then ask them again for money for how many other things is sad and shows what level we were at as a gaming industry if it was a free to play game I can accept that you want to make money somehow but asking for money to play the game and then asking for money for almost every single thing is shameful to say the least.
One last thing I wanted to mention is if you are going to play the game play it with your group if you play this game alone I really wish you good patience on your nerves most players think they can do it all alone they never play in a group because they just don't care so I would say pay close attention to that.
It makes me sad because it was a game I really wanted to release and I did have a good time but I can't support such moves from a company that isn't looking to make their game but is only looking to get you your money.

Bem, Nesse jogo se baseia quem vai ser maior protagonista da partida, literalmente sempre tem aquele cara só porque fez um drible, já acha novo messi desse jogo. Uma pena não trouxeram até data dessa analise crossplay.

Pontos positivos: Mecânica Inovadora praticamente vc joga tudo como se fosse tudo no manual de um fifa, Personalização variável onde você pode montar qualquer tipo personagem sua mente linda possa acracia suas gameplays, servidores br,  Comemorações diferenciadas, Personagens conhecidos e famosos como ronaldinho gaucho.

Pontos negativos: Jogador solo sofre um pouco jogando no modo 3x3, queda servidores, Sem crossplay até dado momento, Atraso de servidor em algumas partidas, Mecânica do carrinho bem bugada, Comunidade em si chat de voz aberta bem toxica, Prologo bem fraco sem modo historia, Treinamento atack, dribles e defesa bem raso, Mecanica Movimentação com camera bem complicado para quem ta começando jogar agora.

Resumindo: Se você é fã de futebol quer viver num bluelock da vida compre esse jogo, contudo, muitas coisas ainda pode aparecer nesse jogo tanto positivo ou negativo. Além mais jogo é bem viciante, eu mesmo tive certos receios no inicio em si.

Recomendo jogar controle por causa é mais confortavel e facil para controle de bola e chutes.

Quem quiser novas amizades so comenta na minha bio adiciono pessoal novo rematch.

Le jeu a un potentiel de dingue : les mécaniques sont cool, la prise en main est agréable, et y a une vraie vibe FIFA Street qui donne des bonnes sensations quand on commence à capter comment ça se joue.

Mais alors niveau technique… c’est pas ça du tout.
Entre les lags serveurs, les désyncs et un netcode aux fraises, c’est souvent plus frustrant qu’amusant. Et quand t’es solo, bonne chance : jouer avec des randoms, c’est souvent galère, tu finis la plupart du temps en défense par défaut. (Arrivé en Platine, ça s’améliore un peu, mais c’est encore léger.)

Le vrai souci, c’est qu’on sent que le jeu n’est pas fini. Clairement, ça aurait mérité une early access. Là, ça donne l’impression d’un lancement un peu trop pressé, et c’est dommage parce que le cœur du gameplay est vraiment cool.

J’y reviendrai sûrement plus tard, mais pour l’instant, difficile de le recommander sans réserve.
Je mets quand même un avis positif, pour le potentiel, mais c'est surtout un avis très mitigé...

Eu tenho me divertido jogando, no entanto, tenho alguns pontos pra elencar pq eu não ''recomendaria'' e espero que façam sentido.

[spoiler] Resumo dos tópicos: Jogo que passa uma vibe muito forte de early access e free 2 play, só que custando 80 reais.[/spoiler]

- Jogo PAGO com o mesmo modelo de um jogo free 2 play: passe de temporada, moeda premium e afins. Até onde eu sei, não existe uma maneira consistente/garantida de acumular moeda premium apenas jogando. Não é um jogo pay 2 win pois os itens são tudo cosméticos/customização, mas considero um modelo incoerente e ganancioso. 

- Tudo aponta que esse jogo foi feito pra console e só portaram pro pc sem levar em conta os padrões de qualidade de vida que jogos no PC têm, por ex. rebindar os comandos do controle de xbox/playstation e ausência de chat de texto. 

- O jogo falta outros recursos básicos de qualidade de vida que qualquer jogo ''competitivo'' tem, como histórico de partidas. No modo ranqueado, você não consegue em nenhum momento ver o rank dos jogadores com quem você está jogando, nem ao final da partida.

- O jogo é supostamente multiplataforma mas não existe nenhuma maneira de você formar times com amigos jogando em outras plataformas, nem mesmo se você joga na steam e seu amigo joga no xbox/gamepass. 

- Já mencionei que vc compra o jogo e ainda precisa pagar pra desbloquear as coisas dentro dele?

REMATCH ist nach langer Zeit mal wieder ein Fußball Spiel was mich beeindruckt hat. Anders als bei Fifa steuert man in Rematch in der 3D Ansicht seinen eigenen Charakter, das Ganze im 3v3 4v4 oder 5v5 Online/ Ranked. Auch eine Kampagne soll wohl folgen. Die Grafik ist sehr hübsch gemacht, dennoch würde ich mich über mehr Details, grade außerhalb des Stadions freuen. Performance technisch lief es aber super und bringt sogar DLSS sowie Framegen mit. Die Steuerung ist vor allem mit Tastatur sehr gewöhnungsbedürftig, vor allem das Passen ist sehr empfindlich. Wenn alles aber dann mal läuft macht es wirklich Spaß. Der Torwart kann im übrigen immer auch während das Spiels gewechselt werden. Vor allem zusammen mit Freunden kann ich es nur empfehlen. Leider gibt es bisher noch kein Crossplay, was aber zeitnah folgen soll. Ich bin gespannt wie Season 1 wird!

The game has great potential: the game loop is fun and addictive, there is a lot of potential for outplaying and putting together nice team goals. Whether it will fulfill it is an entirely different matter. Played the betas, and paid 51$ as a gesture of goodwill because I was hoping that some of the worst net code and game-breaking bugs would be fixed for release. As it turned out the game released in identical state to beta and there was nothing changed other than adding cosmetics and micro-transactions. Also since release, the developers haven't done enough rectify the biggest issues and only added a collaboration with Puma and new fluff. 

People say this a competitor to Rocket League, but nothing could be further from the truth, These games have core gameplay differences: RL is a physics game while Rematch is an aracdey title where your and other characters are locked into animations and 50-50s are decided basically by a coin toss. you can be marking an opponent and a ball is flying toward you, you get the blue square and you input the command for the heading animation to head the ball away, you get locked into the animation and fully expect to win the ball, only for the opponent behind you to jump up and scissor kick it into goal. Tough luck, his animation just happened to win over yours. How? Why? No one knows, it's basically just RNG and there is nothing you can do game knowledge or skill-wise to reliably win 50-50s.  In Rocket League making contact with the ball and the way 50-50s are decided is much more transparent and fair and you know that if something didn't go your way it's because you messed up. 

All the stuff other have said about balls and players teleporting, desync, lack of cross play, lack of ranked matchmaking, ♥♥♥♥♥♥ toxic teammates etc.  is still there despite these problems being there since the earliest betas. This game will need years if it ever hopes to achieve even the fraction of RL's polish. This game turned to be a hit purely because football fans have been crying for a title like this for years, but all we got was FIFA. However, releasing the game in its current state and charging people upwards of 65$ for a supposedly competitive sports game, where the results of matches are deterimed by random ♥♥♥♥♥♥ netcode RNG rather than skill is not responsible. They needed to work on this for several more months, maybe a year, before they could release a half-decent product, which would still be flawed, but much more playable than it is now. But they clearly chose to cash in and time will tell whether this decision would serve them well.

TLDR: In its current state this is a 5/10 with a potential to be 10/10, but I can't recommend Rematch in its current state, which is a shame because I WANT to play this game and find myself coming back to it, but as soon as I do I am reminded of why I left. Save yourself some nerves and money and wait for the fundamental problems this game has to be fixed.

The Problem is not the Game, it's the Players.


Look, I know,I haven’t spent that much time in the game. But let me tell you something universal: every team has that one guy. You know the type. Watches Ronaldo and Messi YouTube Shorts on repeat, hits the pitch thinking he’s Himothy. Spoiler alert: you’re not.

We’ve got three teammates, three! wide open, with clear shots on goal. But no, you decide to channel your inner street baller and pull off some tricks like this is FIFA Street 2006. And of course, like clockwork, you fumble it.

Guess what happens next? Now the ball's rolling back toward our half. Meanwhile, our entire team is still chilling in the enemy box like it’s a vacation spot. And our poor goalie? Left to 1v3 like he's auditioning for the next “Impossible Saves” compilation on YouTube. Gee, I wonder how that turns out?

So I thought, fine. I’ll play defender for a few matches. I’ll stay back, cover our side, be the grown-up. And yeah it kinda works. But you? You don’t learn. You’re still out there trying to recreate the World Cup Final, solo queue edition. Every opportunity we get to actually rank up, you throw it away like a last-minute crossbar challenge.

Because God forbid you pass the ball… in a team-based game.

Thank you for listening. Please add Chat ingame so i can tell people they need to learn how to pass and stop fumbling their tricks, thank you.

Positives:
-When it clicks it's a fun experience 

Negatives: 
- Netcode is awful and will have the ball just faze through you or just straight up lie that you've mad contact 
- Most random players are brain dead and will just ball chase forcing you to play defence (I have 19 goals to 900+ interceptions) 
- Ranked isn't ranked at all. if you're in silver you can end up facing team in platinum 
- There is almost no communication options if you arent't using a mic. All you have is Good Job, Sorry, Thanks and We got this. No "Defend", No "Watch left or Right side" and No "Stop being selfish" 

Overall it's not worth it for the price.

Originalpost zu Season 0:

Als Fussballbegeisterter, der mit Pro Evolution Soccer 5+6 und FIFA aufgewachsen ist, war ich im Jahr 2015 skeptisch über ein neues Spiel namens Rocket League. Fussball mit Autos - das kann eigentlich nichts sein. Trotzdem hatte es mich damals in seinen Bann gezogen aufgrund der leicht zu lernenden Steuerung und der Sogwirkung aufgrund der schnellen, kurzen Partien. Und doch hatte ich mir immer ein Spiel gewünscht, welches die Essenz des Fussballs besser abbildet. Sloclap hat dies nun endlich geschafft! Die gleiche Sogwirkung durch kurzweilige Partien und mit einer leicht zu lernenden Steuerung, aber nun als tatsächliches (arcadiges) Fussballspiel. Rematch mag zwar ein geringeres Skill-Ceiling haben als Rocket League aber im Gegensatz zu RL kann man hier z.B. durch Fussballsachverstand und Teamwork auch skilltechnisch wesentlich stärkere Gegner besiegen. Es gibt zwar noch technische Schwächen vor allem im Netcode aber den Spaß, den man vor allem mit einem eingespielten 5er Team hat, beeinträchtigt das kaum. Sehr Empfehlenswert!

Do yourself a favor and don't buy this game. This is an early access game, not a full release. No cross play (even though they said this game was going to have cross play), very bad crashes and lag on high end machines, and only one ranked mode (5v5). The mechanics and moves are cool but what absolutely destroys this game (besides the horrible netcode) is the community. Unless you are playing 3v3, it is absolutely needed for the entire team to be passing to each other in order to succeed / win the game. There are so many players that do not understand this or do not care about passing. Not passing is an immediate loss for your team. If the devs want to make this an enjoyable game, they need to incentivize passing and punish players that hold on to the ball too long. I really want to see this game succeed, but I have very low faith that it will

edit: I have never experienced playing with a more toxic and disgusting player base than this game has and I grew up in black ops 2 lobbies on the 360. This game genuinely brings out the absolute worst in people. It so heavily requires teamwork to win, and I have been in multiple games where my teammates purposely sabotage our games. Do yourself a favor and never play or buy this game

Après une cinquantaine d’heures de jeu, surtout en solo, je ne peux pas recommander ce jeu dans l’état : il y a trop de choses qui ne vont pas.

Points positifs

- Gameplay fun et dynamique

- Mécaniques bien pensées

- Très amusant en team avec des joueurs du même niveau

- Graphismes simples mais efficaces

Points négatifs

- Le système de passe est totalement bancal : sur clavier/souris, un léger effleurement et la trajectoire part dans tous les sens.

- Les devs ont été très actifs les 3–4 premiers jours avec des MàJ quotidiennes, puis… plus rien (par contre la boutique et les partenariats, eux, bougent toujours).

- Lags et désync rendent beaucoup de parties exécrables.

- Solo en classé = enfer : Vous pouvez tomber contre des équipes très déséquilibrées. De plus, il suffit qu’un seul joueur faible soit dans votre groupe pour bloquer la progression. Et comme vous pouvez inviter des amis de niveau inférieur au vôtre, il n’est pas rare de vous retrouver avec du silver contre du platine (ou plus) – c’est complètement ridicule.

- Les parties “normales” sont polluées par des joueurs au skin Ronaldinho qui ne passent jamais le ballon.

- En classé, si vous tombez dans un groupe de 4, vous vous retrouvez systématiquement au goal au début du match: ça m'est arrivé 9 fois de suite, et pour la moitié des matchs je suis resté coincé dans la cage…

- En outre, le comportement toxique des joueurs — qui spam de « good job » à chaque petite erreur — nuit profondément à l’expérience. Je recommande de désactiver le chat vocal pour quiconque souhaite limiter cette toxicité.

- Le jeu est payant et propose en plus des microtransactions en boutique, certains objets n’étant disponibles qu’en argent réel. Si la qualité avait été au rendez-vous, cela se justifierait par la taille du studio, mais ce n’est malheureusement pas le cas.

Le titre possède un potentiel indéniable, mais je ne prends actuellement plus aucun plaisir à y jouer seul. L’expérience solo s’avère particulièrement frustrante, même si le jeu peut sans doute être plus agréable entre amis.
Par ailleurs, le système de passes rend le jeu extrêmement frustrant : malgré des centaines de retours de la communauté, rien n’a changé à ce jour.

Si des améliorations significatives sont apportées, je réviserai volontiers mon avis. En l’état, je vous recommande d’être vigilant avant l’achat.

Rematch tries to be a good game. Quick matches, a sprinkle of tactics, easy to jump into. And at first, you think: "Hey, this could be fun." But then reality kicks in – hard.

The controls are absolute garbage. Passing feels like a dice roll, player movement is sluggish, and inputs randomly vanish into the void. Add random lag spikes and frame drops, and you've got a recipe for controller-smashing frustration – especially when it hits during a crucial play.

But the real kicker? The character design. It's like the devs set out to create the gayest, most flamboyant caricatures possible just to tick diversity boxes and virtue signal on Twitter. It's not inclusive – it's obnoxious. Feels more like satire, except they’re dead serious. Woke for the sake of being woke, with no subtlety, no taste, and definitely no player agency.

Bottom line: There's a decent game buried under all this performative trash. But with its broken mechanics and forced aesthetics, Rematch squanders its potential fast.

This was my first review:
"Best soccer game ever, the potential is huge, there will be tournments, i hope to see in the future some kind of championship formula"

Now that i'm Elite (max rank) i have to change my review:
I'm very disappointed about the fact that devs are considering keeping well knowed glitches/exploit those they call “emergent mechanics”, the milestones are "Ippy Slide", "Golden Boost"  and  "Blade Shot" because some players consider them a way to make the game "faster" and more "skill based". I'm going straight to the point:

those mechanics are the CANCER of the game, there is NO SKILL using an exploit that teleports the ball or spam an almost unblockable shot that takes 1 second to be performed.

There are tons of way to make dribbling deeper, variegate same for ball control both in order to make the game looks faster. An example is to add more than 4 dribling directions, making it x3 moves instead of x2 with longer distances traveled by the ball. 

Right now endgame is a mess with people not playing as a team, only trying to exploit as best as they can those glitches, ball (and bodies) teleporting everywhere, 90% unblockable blade shots, i already know tons of people quitting the game because of this.

So Sloclap don't be fooled by those who tell you that by removing the aforementioned mechanics the game would suffer in terms of skill, depth and speed BECAUSE IT IS NOT TRUE, THOSE MECHANICS ARE KILLING THE GAME.
сигмоэгоисто минетомоменто

I'm giving a negative review in hopes the developer see this.
After putting some hours in this game i can give positive and negative feedback. While this is season 0 right now it feels nothing at all finished in many corners. When I compare it to Marvel rivals (what is not really fair because this is the first multiplayer from the dev team) i can say that their season 0 really felt like a season before finfish. But now to my points:

Lets start with the positives:
The gameplay and short matches are awesome. Its action and tactic that can change very fast.
The moveset is really good, you can do all the simple football stuff and because of some non existing rules and walls around the stadium you can do a lot of tricks and plays. 
Overall the gameplay feels very dynamic and free.
The ranked up to now is handled different which i very much like. Instead of ranking up and down thru different ranks you only do that in your current rank like Bronze, Silver, Gold, etc.  But to rank up is also harder because you need to win 2 out of 3 matches after getting enough ranked points.
The options to decorate your profile and create your charakter are good enough i would say, i personelly would wish for more face creativity but thats just me.


Now to Negative stuff:
I dont really now where to start. A lot of small things that while small is something i just cant oversee.
A lot, and i mean a lot of times the UI of the games doesnt really work. After matches you get shown that you are lvl 0 or in the menu. I doesnt show how much you xp you gained for battlepass or your own level.
A lot of desync. I heard about some gamebreaking ones but up to now i had minor and one where i apperantly dissapeard and couldnt touch the ball for 15s.
The server connection is a hand in hand problem i guess with the desync but me and my friends had multiple encounters where we got kicked out and had to reconnect.
Not everything but many things in the UI are not good to navigate thru. I took me several visits to the character creator to discover all the options.
Gameplay wise i can say that the hitboxes are still very inconsistent. Sometimes the goalkeeper catches the ball with the tip of his feet and sometimes his whole body flys thru the ball. 
After matches finish and you get the screen with 3 sliders its really bad. I takes time to show xp and sometimes if you go to the scoreboard you cant slide back and look at anything else. Sometimes you automaticlly get slided back when you slide to the scoreboard or just to the xp. This endscreen overall is really bad right now.
The achievements are bugged.
There are bugs where its that you shoot or pass the ball but for its just the same animation over and over and for others you just run past the ball.

A critic point for me is the controls. I would have liked the slide and tackle to be different buttons. It was said to come custom controls so thats just extra.


Closing remarks:
This game has potential, i believe and hope the server issues get fixed, that the ui issues with wrong infos gets corrected and that they overthrow some of the ui and make it better accessible or better to overlook at least. I dont really have critic for the gameplay, only that hitboxes are inconsistent which could also be because of the connection.
For me peronally i can only say that right now it feels more like a pre season 0 wich im very sad about. I will keep playing and see if they can fix everything.

Rematch is a great game that manages to capture the thrill and intensity of football like none other,the controls feel smooth and responsive—exactly what you'd want from a game that thrives on quick decision-making and precision.

As you climb the ranks you will need to improve your spatial awareness and be more mechanically precise with your passes/shots otherwise it could cost you the match resulting in a heartbreaking defeat.
The game could use a few more tweaks and a little bit of polishing in its current state which will hopefully come with future patches.

If you're a football enjoyer like myself - I can only recommend you this rare gem of a sports game.

Here are a few glitches I’ve noticed in the game.

1. Disappearing Ball
At times during matches, the ball reaches your player but remains invisible.

2. Frozen Character
Sometimes when you join a match, your character gets stuck and can only walk. You can’t pass, shoot, sprint, or do anything else.

3. Party/Squad Formation Problems
With a weak or unstable internet connection, creating a party or squad becomes difficult and frustrating.

4. Goalkeeper “Catch” Glitch
Rarely, even when the goalkeeper visibly catches the ball, the goal is still awarded. This bug is also noticeable in replays after a goal is scored.

5. Persistent Mic Audio
Occasionally, players’ microphone audio continues playing in-game even after muting it in audio settings.

Even with these bugs, the game is still super exciting and really fun to play ، especially if you play it with your friends !
Hope they fix these bugs soon !

um jogo extremamente divertido, principalmente quando jogado em equipe. A experiência coletiva é o que mais brilha aqui,  jogar com amigos ou até com desconhecidos deixa as partidas muito mais intensas e viciantes. Em apenas uma semana, joguei cerca de 80 horas, o que mostra o quanto o jogo prende a atenção. A vontade de grindar para alcançar o modo ranqueado é constante, e o sistema de progressão consegue manter o jogador motivado.

Por outro lado, o ambiente competitivo pode ser um pouco tóxico, especialmente se você estiver com o chat de voz ativado. Xingamentos e até comentários preconceituosos acontecem com certa frequência, o que pode incomodar bastante quem não tem muita paciência ou não curte esse tipo de clima. Felizmente, é possível silenciar os outros jogadores, o que ameniza bastante o problema — mas é algo a se considerar antes de mergulhar nas partidas com comunicação aberta.

Mesmo com uma proposta simples, Rematch consegue se destacar visualmente e entregar uma jogabilidade sólida. As mecânicas são fáceis de aprender, mas difíceis de dominar, o que mantém o jogo interessante por muito tempo. Espero que as próximas atualizações tragam ainda mais conteúdo de qualidade e que a comunidade cresça de forma saudável, porque o potencial do jogo é enorme.

"Rematch is a very enjoyable game! I played for 5 hours and had a great time with its gameplay mechanics. The controls feel smooth, the matches are intense, and it really keeps you engaged. Whether you're playing solo or with friends, it offers solid competitive fun. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fast-paced action experience."

I really wanted to love this game, but unfortunately the controls are just... something man.

You can either play with mouse and keyboard or controller, but both feel bad for different reasons. If you decide to go with mouse and keyboard, you will soon learn that passing the ball does NOT use the same crosshair shooting does, which is counter intuitive but okay. I can understand why they made this, so that you can pass without looking to where you wanna pass (I do feel like a better solution would be to have passing without looking being only when you hold the L2 stance, but anyways).

So how do you pass? First instinct is to think it's tied to the direction you are holding on your keyboard, your movement direction, but that's not true either. The strangest part is that that's how passing on the controller works, but not on the keyboard, probably because the WASD doesn't allow for the same range of angles to pass, but even with that limitation I would have GLADLY preferred to at least have the option to pass like this over the garbage they invented instead.

So here's how you pass with a keyboard and mouse: You have to flick the mouse to the direction you are passing. It doesn't matter where you are looking, it doesn't matter where you are moving towards, it's a somewhat fast mouse flick that passes the ball. I found that not only extremely counter intuitive and weird but also very annoying to control. I feel like there are plenty of other solutions and this one just stinks so much.

So really, if you want to play this game and have fun, your best bet is to use a controller. But that's where I was stuck at and ended up refunding the game because of it: The controller is also not that good either. Sure, it's better than M&K, but shooting on the controller just feels really bad. First reason is of course aiming with a controller is always a limitation compared to the mouse and keyboard, but also there is one another issue.

To make the ball curve on shooting you need to aim, press the R2, hold it and WHILE you are holding for half a second, you need to also choose a direction with the right thumbstick and hold it there meanwhile. The problem is that for some reason on the controller this also moves the camera slightly, becase right thumbstick is also camera controls, which will move the crosshair off where you first aimed, to a place you didn't want to aim at.

The weird thing is that this doesn't happen with mouse and keyboard. I don't know why. The crosshair locks once you start charging the shot, but not with the controller. You CAN minimize this issue on the settings of controller, but it's always there at least a little bit.

So yeah, for a game about soccer you really need to have perfect controls and I don't think this game has it. It's also very bgugy at the moment, but I'm sure they will fix it with time. I hope devs listen because I've heard quite a lot of people complaining about the controls, and I really want to like this game, but at the moment this is an easy refund for me.

I didn’t expect to enjoy Rematch as much as I do, but it quickly became one of my favorite multiplayer games this year. It's like if street football and a fighting game had a baby—with flashy moves, smooth movement, and just enough chaos to keep every match exciting. The controls took me a little time to get used to, but once they clicked, the gameplay felt incredibly fluid and rewarding.

One of the best parts is how important teamwork is. You can’t just run in solo and hope to score. Passing, positioning, and timing your tackles or dashes make all the difference. And when a good play comes together, it feels amazing. There's a lot of room to improve and get creative with your style.

Visually, the game stands out with its futuristic, colorful arenas and animated characters. It doesn’t try to be hyper-realistic, and I appreciate that. The soundtrack also keeps the energy high throughout the match.

There are a few rough edges, especially with matchmaking and the occasional lag, but overall it hasn’t ruined the fun for me. I’m really excited to see where the devs take it next. If you're into fast-paced, competitive games, Rematch is absolutely worth checking out.

I have a love/hate relationship with this game. 

The beta made me have this feeling something that I haven't felt since 2018 with Fortnite. That feeling being a game that me and the boys stay up into the late hours of the night saying "alright, just one more game.",as we proceed to play another 10. REMATCH somehow finds a way to blend the mechanical aspects of something like rocket league, and the technical/tactical aspects of Pro Clubs in FIFA (or the worse EAFC) and creates, what a times can be, a football fans dream.

However, is has some major flaws. Now currently I have 12.4 hours played since release, and around another 12-15 in the beta. So this is coming from the view of a newcomer. I'm not one of those guys who already had 200+ hours in the beta lol.

1. The game is incredibly janky.

The amount of times where the ball gets stuck on the ground and you have to wait for someone to kick it, NOT touch it but shoot it, in order for the ball to reset. Or when you see the goalie make a save as they dive into their own net (this may be a design choice from the developers and I've started to accept that, but it still feels jank). Or something like seeing yourself win the ball, only for it to rubberband back to the opposition. Some of this "jank" could be a skill issue, but it doesn't stop the fact that feels terrible when it happens.

I love games that are difficult, and make you feel as if you must take a course in order to full understand it. Then reward you for learning those mechanics. REMATCH can do that at times, but it has moments that truly make you question if some of those very mechanics are even suppose to function the way they currently do at all.

Also the lack of being able to customize controls is criminal.

2. The community is less football fans and more Blue Lock enjoyers.

Now there is nothing wrong with being on either side. However the "Blade Shot" epidemic that has taken over the game is terrible. You rarely see build up play or evening passing for that matter. Everyone is trying to rainbow flick into a extra effort bicycle kick.

3. Currently no crossplay.

I know this is coming soon and is a priority for the devs, but it still sucks to not have.

TLDR:

So do I recommend this game? Answer: Maybe?

The game has incredible potential, and I truly want it to be nothing short of great. However the game has some crucial issues like it's jankiness, the community, and it launching without crossplay that makes it's not shine as much as it should.

Rematch od Sloclap to prawdziwy powiew świeżości wśród sportowych gier wciągający, dynamiczny i pełen emocji tryb 3v3, 4v4 lub 5v5, który świetnie sprawdza się zarówno w meczu z kumplami, jak i w rywalizacji solo. Kontrola postaci, manewry, precyzyjne strzały i intensywny gameplay dają niesamowitą satysfakcję; przywołują trochę klimat Rocket League ale stopą, nie samochodem. Klimatyczna oprawa wizualna sprawia, że gra po prostu cieszy oko. Niestety, debiut w dniu premiery był trochę nierówny. Tysiące graczy wbiło w ciągu 24 h, a duże obciążenie serwerów uwypukliło problemy z synchronizacją piłki, zacinki, bugi z kamerką i freeze’y. Momentami gra sprawiała wrażenie „pół Early Access”. W nielicznych przypadkach po golu zdarzały się crashe i znikające piłki potrafiły skutecznie odebrać radość z rozgrywki. Do tego doszedł brak cross‑playu, co rozczarowało wielu graczy liczących na wspólną zabawę z przyjaciółmi na PS5 czy Xboxie. Gracze na padach narzekają też na brak możliwości remapowania oraz sztywno ustawioną kamerę choć twórcy zapowiedzieli poprawki. Z drugiej strony, Sloclap nie siedzi z założonymi rękami. Niemal codzienne hotfixy poprawiają stabilność, synchronizację, crashe i eliminują najbardziej absurdalne bugi. Komunikacja ze studiem jest szybka i konkretna, co daje nadzieję na szybkie dopracowanie tytułu.
Podsumowując Rematch to kawał znakomicie udanego, energetycznego sportu online. Gra ma ogromny potencjał i na dłuższą metę naprawdę wciąga szczególnie jeśli grasz w zgranym teamie. W dniu premiery doświadczałem jednak frustrujących momentów z lagami, bugami i fazującą synchronizacją. Na szczęście deweloperzy pracują nad tym non stop, więc wierzę, że niedługo będzie blisko ideału. Na razie gorąco polecam każdemu fanowi intensywnych meczy, ale kupujcie świadomi, że to premiera z drobnymi, choć irytującymi niedoróbkami.
